old_claim.note: LED Lighting (Efficient Illumination) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because pulse oximetry uses red/infrared optical absorption and predates the high-efficiency blue/white LED lighting node.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical review section on Aoyagi’s 1972 pulse oximetry discovery and red/infrared absorption method.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review places pulse oximetry in the early 1970s and describes the method around optical absorption in red and infrared spectral regions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That mechanism supports an optics predecessor but not a dependency on efficient blue/white LED lighting, which this graph scopes to 1993 high-brightness LED capability.

ontology_before: Pulse oximetry depended on a later efficient-lighting node, incorrectly making a 1970s medical monitor require 1990s high-brightness LED lighting.
ontology_after: Pulse oximetry now depends on early optics, clinical medicine, and digital signal processing, while later LED improvements can remain downstream refinements.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The LED node were split and this dependency were retargeted to an earlier red or infrared LED node.
The pulse_oximetry node were narrowed to post-1993 fingertip oximeters that specifically require high-brightness LEDs.
A primary device source showed the 1972 pulse oximetry invention depended on high-efficiency LED lighting as scoped here.
short_reason: Pulse oximetry predates high-efficiency LED lighting.
